summary: The origin of slow wave potentials is attributed to the interstitial cells of Cajal within the gastrointestinal tract. These specialized cells act as intrinsic pacemakers responsible for rhythmically generating electrical activity essential for coordinating gut contractions through propagation into surrounding smooth muscle layers.
